UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores personal and biometric data for an athlete, enabling nutrition and training calculations.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: The athlete's email address, used for authentication (but not stored here).

dateOfBirth.description: The athlete's date of birth, used for age-related calculations (e.g., BMR).

gender.description: The athlete's biological gender (e.g., 'male', 'female'), used for BMR calculation.

activityLevel.description: The athlete's general activity level (e.g., 'Sedentario', 'Ligero', 'Moderado', 'Fuerte', 'Atleta'), used to calculate TDEE.

athleticDiscipline.description: The primary combat discipline of the athlete (e.g., 'MMA', 'BJJ', 'Muay Thai'), used for specific energy expenditure calculations.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

BiometricMeasurement.title: BiometricMeasurement
BiometricMeasurement.description: Records historical biometric data for an athlete, used for trend analysis and calculations.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BiometricMeasurement entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile this measurement belongs to. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N BiometricMeasurement)

measurementDate.description: The date and time when the biometric measurement was taken.

weightKg.description: The athlete's weight in kilograms at the time of measurement.

bodyFatPercentage.description: The athlete's body fat percentage at the time of measurement.

muscleMassPercentage.description: The athlete's muscle mass percentage at the time of measurement.

notes.description: Any additional notes or observations related to this measurement.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the biometric measurement record was created.

FoodItem.title: FoodItem
FoodItem.description: Represents an item in the public food catalog with its detailed nutritional information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FoodItem entity, typically from the public '/alimentos' collection.

name.description: The name of the food item.

description.description: A brief description of the food item.

category.description: The category of the food item (e.g., 'Frutas', 'Carnes', 'Cereales').

caloriesPer100g.description: The number of calories per 100 grams of the food item.

proteinPer100g.description: The amount of protein in grams per 100 grams of the food item.

fatPer100g.description: The amount of fat in grams per 100 grams of the food item.

carbohydratesPer100g.description: The amount of carbohydrates in grams per 100 grams of the food item.

fiberPer100g.description: The amount of fiber in grams per 100 grams of the food item.

sugarPer100g.description: The amount of sugar in grams per 100 grams of the food item.

sodiumPer100g.description: The amount of sodium in milligrams per 100 grams of the food item.

imageUrl.description: URL to an image of the food item.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the food item record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the food item record was last updated.

MealLog.title: MealLog
MealLog.description: Records a single meal consumed by an athlete, summarizing its nutritional content.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MealLog entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who logged this meal. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N MealLog)

logDate.description: The date and time when the meal was consumed.

totalCalories.description: The total estimated calories for this meal, derived from its consumed food items.

totalProtein.description: The total estimated protein in grams for this meal.

totalFat.description: The total estimated fat in grams for this meal.

totalCarbohydrates.description: The total estimated carbohydrates in grams for this meal.

notes.description: Any additional notes or observations about the meal.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the meal log was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the meal log was last updated.

FoodConsumption.title: FoodConsumption
FoodConsumption.description: Details a specific food item consumed as part of a MealLog.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FoodConsumption entity.

mealLogId.description: Reference to the MealLog this food item belongs to. (Relationship: MealLog 1:N FoodConsumption)

foodItemId.description: Reference to the FoodItem that was consumed. (Relationship: FoodItem 1:N FoodConsumption)

quantityGrams.description: The quantity of the food item consumed in grams.

consumedCalories.description: The calculated calories from this specific food consumption.

consumedProtein.description: The calculated protein in grams from this specific food consumption.

consumedFat.description: The calculated fat in grams from this specific food consumption.

consumedCarbohydrates.description: The calculated carbohydrates in grams from this specific food consumption.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the food consumption record was created.

TrainingSession.title: TrainingSession
TrainingSession.description: Records a single training activity performed by an athlete.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TrainingSession entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who logged this training session. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N TrainingSession)

logDate.description: The date and time when the training session occurred.

activityType.description: The type of training activity (e.g., 'MMA', 'BJJ', 'Muay Thai', 'Strength Training', 'Cardio').

durationMinutes.description: The duration of the training session in minutes.

intensityLevel.description: The perceived intensity level of the training session (e.g., 'Baja', 'Moderada', 'Alta').

estimatedCaloriesBurned.description: The estimated number of calories burned during this session.

notes.description: Any additional notes or observations about the training session.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the training session record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the training session record was last updated.

Recipe.title: Recipe
Recipe.description: Stores detailed information about an AI-generated (or user-saved) tactical recipe.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Recipe entity.

description.description: A brief description of the recipe.

instructions.description: Detailed steps for preparing the recipe.

prepTimeMinutes.description: The estimated preparation time for the recipe in minutes.

cookTimeMinutes.description: The estimated cooking time for the recipe in minutes.

servings.description: The number of servings the recipe yields.

estimatedProteinPerServing.description: Estimated protein in grams per serving.

estimatedFatPerServing.description: Estimated fat in grams per serving.

estimatedCarbohydratesPerServing.description: Estimated carbohydrates in grams per serving.

technicalAnalysis.description: AI-generated nutritional and physiological analysis of the recipe.

imageUrl.description: URL to an image of the prepared recipe.

generatedByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who generated or saved this recipe. (Relationship: UserProfile 0:1 Recipe)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the recipe record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the recipe record was last updated.

RecipeIngredient.title: RecipeIngredient
RecipeIngredient.description: Details a specific food item used as an ingredient in a Recipe.

id.description: Unique identifier for the RecipeIngredient entity.

recipeId.description: Reference to the Recipe this ingredient belongs to. (Relationship: Recipe 1:N RecipeIngredient)

foodItemId.description: Reference to the FoodItem used as an ingredient. (Relationship: FoodItem 1:N RecipeIngredient)

quantityValue.description: The numeric value of the quantity for the ingredient (e.g., '2' for '2 cups').

unit.description: The unit of measurement for the ingredient (e.g., 'grams', 'ml', 'cups', 'units').

notes.description: Any additional preparation notes for the ingredient (e.g., 'chopped', 'sliced').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the recipe ingredient record was created.

definition.description: Stores personal and biometric data for an athlete. This document's ID (`userId`) must match `request.auth.uid` for authorization. Includes `id` field matching the document ID.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Records historical biometric data for a specific athlete. The `userId` in the path is used for authorization, ensuring only the owner can access their measurements. Includes denormalized 'userId' field for robust authorization.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: biometricMeasurementId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific biometric measurement record.

definition.description: Publicly accessible catalog of food items with nutritional information. This collection is read-only for all users (authenticated or unauthenticated), supporting global lookup for meal logging and recipe creation.

params.name: foodItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific food item.

definition.description: Records a single meal consumed by an athlete. The `userId` in the path ensures private access for the owner. Includes denormalized 'userId' field for robust authorization.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: mealLogId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific meal log.

definition.description: Details a specific food item consumed as part of a MealLog. Ownership is inherited from the parent MealLog via the path structure, ensuring authorization independence. Includes `mealLogId` field matching the parent ID.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: mealLogId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent meal log.

params.name: foodConsumptionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific food consumption entry within a meal log.

definition.description: Records a single training activity performed by an athlete. The `userId` in the path ensures private access for the owner. Includes denormalized 'userId' field for robust authorization.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: trainingSessionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific training session.

definition.description: Stores AI-generated or user-saved tactical recipes. The `userId` in the path ensures private access for the owner. Includes denormalized 'generatedByUserId' field for robust authorization.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: recipeId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific recipe.

definition.description: Details a specific food item used as an ingredient in a Recipe. Ownership is inherited from the parent Recipe via the path structure, ensuring authorization independence. Includes `recipeId` field matching the parent ID.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: recipeId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent recipe.

params.name: recipeIngredientId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific ingredient within a recipe.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed following the core principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Access Modeling to ensure security, scalability, and debuggability.

**Authorization Independence:** All user-specific data, including UserProfile, BiometricMeasurements, MealLogs, FoodConsumptions, TrainingSessions, Recipes, and RecipeIngredients, is organized hierarchically under `/perfiles/{userId}/`. This path-based ownership ensures that authorization can be determined solely by matching `request.auth.uid` with the `{userId}` wildcard in the path. For example, for documents in `/perfiles/{userId}/biometricMeasurements/{biometricMeasurementId}`, the rule `allow read, write: if request.auth.uid == userId;` is sufficient and robust. For subcollections, such as `foodConsumptions` nested under `mealLogs` (e.g., `/perfiles/{userId}/mealLogs/{mealLogId}/foodConsumptions/{foodConsumptionId}`), the `userId` from the top-level path segment is inherently carried down. This means no `get()` calls are required in security rules to check the ownership of parent documents when accessing child documents, thereby eliminating hierarchical authorization dependencies. This design is critical for supporting atomic operations (transactions/batches) and simplifies rule debugging.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
1.  **Public Data (`/alimentos`):** The `/alimentos` collection, representing the public food catalog, is a top-level collection. It has a homogeneous security posture, allowing public read access. This structure inherently supports efficient `list` operations for all users (authenticated or unauthenticated) without requiring any client-side or rule-based filtering, directly addressing the QAP principle.
2.  **Private Data (`/perfiles/{userId}/...`):** For all user-owned data, the hierarchical path `/perfiles/{userId}/...` ensures that `list` operations are implicitly secure. When a user queries `/perfiles/{request.auth.uid}/mealLogs`, Firestore's security rules will only grant access to documents under that specific user's path. This prevents users from inadvertently accessing or listing data belonging to other users, fulfilling the QAP principle for private data by making authorization explicit in the path rather than relying on query filters.

**Structural Segregation:** Data with different security requirements is strictly segregated into distinct collections. For instance, public food items are in `/alimentos`, while private user profiles and their associated logs are under `/perfiles/{userId}/`. This prevents mixing data that requires different access levels in the same collection, making security rules simpler and less prone to errors.
